strategies.name: Fidelity Envelopes + SMA

strengths: Mean reversion logic works well in ranging markets
weaknesses: Envelope width fixed, doesn't adapt to volatility
15m expiry can miss fast reversals or get stopped early
technical_critique.reality_check: Classic mean reversion; works ~60% in tight ranges but deadly in trends. Need ADX/ATR filter to avoid trending phases.

notes: Use as reversal setup near extremes
Avoid trading during major macro news
CRITICAL: Add trend filter or lose on volatile days

strategies.name: Indirect Volume + Price Action

call: Bears power below zero and rising
put: Bears power above zero and falling

strengths: Price action + momentum + oscillators = solid foundation
Works well during EU/US session overlaps
weaknesses: Too many conditions can cause missed entries
Bulls/Bears power lags price, late signals in fast moves
Stochastic+RSI both oscillators = redundant in some cases
technical_critique.reality_check: Professional approach but over-engineered for 5m OTC. Simplify to RSI+S/R for speed. Bulls/Bears power adds value only in slow grinds.

notes: Works better in sessions with stable liquidity
CRITICAL: Don't force S/R where it doesn't exist clearly

strategies.name: MACD Profitunity

call: AO zero-line or bowl bullish pattern
put: AO zero-line or bowl bearish pattern

strengths: AO zero-line cross is clean momentum signal
weaknesses: Fractals repaint until confirmed (2 bars later)
Works best in trending markets, not chop
technical_critique.reality_check: Legit strategy but overcomplicates for OTC speed. Use AO zero-cross + fractal break only. Skip bowl pattern (too slow). Better on 15m than 5m.

notes: CRITICAL: Wait 2 bars for fractal confirmation to avoid repainting

strategies.name: RSI + SMA + Stochastic

call: Fast SMA crosses above slow SMA
put: Fast SMA crosses below slow SMA

weaknesses: RSI and Stochastic are redundant (both oscillators)
SMA cross lags heavily, signals come too late
Catches falling knives in strong trends
Original note suggests 10-15% position = GAMBLING, not trading
Will lose consecutively in trending crypto/commodities
technical_critique.reality_check: DANGEROUS as written. Remove SMA cross, use RSI OR Stochastic (not both). Max 2% risk. This is marketed to gamblers, not sustainable traders. Works only in tight forex ranges.

notes: Original note suggests large position size
For this project, cap risk per trade to 1-3%
CRITICAL: This is a high-risk gamble setup, not professional strategy
Skip SMA cross entirely for OTC speed

strategies.name: EMA 21 + ADX + AO

strengths: ADX filter is GOLD for avoiding chop (best tool here)
Two AO bars reduce whipsaws vs single-bar signals
Low risk due to trend alignment
weaknesses: ADX > 25 is conservative (may miss early trends)
1-2 candle expiry too short for 5m if entry is late
AO can give late signals after momentum already peaked
technical_critique.reality_check: BEST STRATEGY in this list. Professional approach: trend filter + momentum confirmation. ADX alone saves 50% of bad trades. Extend expiry to 2-3 candles for safety.

notes: RECOMMENDED: This is the safest and most professional setup here
Consider ADX > 20 for more signals, or keep > 25 for quality

meta.analyst_notes: Strategies reviewed with critical trading lens. Added market condition filters, asset compatibility, and technical critique. Most channel strategies work in specific conditions only - universal approach will fail. Always backtest per asset class.
